subject: Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the meaning and importance of minerals
Identify different modes of occurrence of minerals
Differentiate between ferrous and non-ferrous minerals
Recognise the distribution of important minerals in India
Understand different types of conventional energy resources
Learn about renewable or non-conventional energy sources
Develop awareness about conservation of minerals and energy resources

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Minerals
topics_covered: Importance of minerals in daily life
teaching_strategy: Discussion on objects made from minerals
student_activities: List household items made from minerals
Identify the minerals used in these products

lesson_plan.title: Mode of Occurrence of Minerals
topics_covered: Minerals in igneous and metamorphic rocks
teaching_strategy: Use of textbook images and maps
student_activities: Draw simple diagrams showing mineral occurrence
Identify examples of minerals from each type

lesson_plan.title: Ferrous and Non-Ferrous Minerals
student_activities: Prepare a table comparing ferrous and non-ferrous minerals

lesson_plan.title: Distribution of Minerals in India
student_activities: Locate mineral regions on the map of India

lesson_plan.title: Conventional Sources of Energy
student_activities: List uses of different energy resources

lesson_plan.title: Non-Conventional Energy Sources
student_activities: Prepare a chart of renewable energy sources

lesson_plan.title: Conservation of Minerals and Energy
student_activities: Write ways to conserve energy at home

teaching_aids: Projector or images for renewable energy
teacher_tip: Use maps and real-life examples of energy use to help students understand how mineral and energy resources influence industrial and economic development.
